question: Build Tier 1 self-repair infrastructure (self-quarantine, failure-signature router, pattern propagation) now as empty/inert code, or hold until Tier 0 has accumulated real production data, per docs/SELF_REPAIR_DOCTRINE.md Part 2's explicit counter-warning against speculative building?

rounds.human_summary: Building Tier 1 infrastructure now risks misaligned resources and potential rework if Tier 0 data reveals unexpected needs. Consider waiting for meaningful data to ensure user-aligned decisions.
rounds.note: prediction.option in this round diverged (predicted Adam might lean 'build now' under momentum pressure, 0.7 confidence) -- prediction is a model of what Adam might choose, not the chair's own recommendation. Asked for a clarifying follow-up round to force the chair's own judgment rather than a prediction of Adam's psychology.

rounds.human_summary: The value here lies in ensuring that resources are not wasted on speculative efforts that may need reworking.
rounds.prediction_option: hold until Tier 0 has real data

consensus: HOLD — do not build Tier 1-3 self-repair infrastructure now. Wait until Tier 0 (Era 1, all 8 stores/gates, shipped 2026-07-20) has accumulated real production data to pull from. Matches docs/SELF_REPAIR_DOCTRINE.md Part 2's explicit counter-warning: 'Tiers 1-3 are pulled by real pain and accumulating data, never pushed speculatively -- pushing them is itself an SO-003 violation.'
reason_no_further_escalation: Two consecutive chair rounds converged on the same answer; per founder instruction, SENTRY escalation is only needed if chair can't reach consensus
routing_context: Founder explicitly declined to arbitrate this scope question directly and instructed routing technical/architectural judgment calls through Chair (and SENTRY if needed) instead of asking him -- see docs/AGENT_INBOX.md pattern extended to solo judgment calls.
